extra.reasoning:
The monotonicity property requires that flipping dizziness from false to true (all other inputs fixed) never lowers acuity. It is violated.

dizziness appears in exactly one rule: interaction_drop (priority 100) IF (dyspnea AND fever AND dizziness) THEN acuity := self_care. self_care is the LOWEST acuity value, and priority 100 is the highest in the rule set, so whenever this rule fires it dominates every other rule.

Counterexample transition with dyspnea=true, fever=true, sweating=false, leg_swelling=false, back_pain=false:

BEFORE (dizziness=false): interaction_drop does not fire. Matching rules on acuity are raise_dyspnea (prio 2 -> urgent) and raise_fever (prio 3 -> emergent). Highest priority is raise_fever, so acuity = emergent.

AFTER (dizziness=true): interaction_drop now fires (dyspnea AND fever AND dizziness all true) with priority 100, beating raise_fever (prio 3) and raise_dyspnea (prio 2). acuity = self_care.

acuity drops from emergent to self_care when dizziness is set true, lowering acuity. No higher-priority rule exists to override interaction_drop (100 is the max priority). The witness_assignment records the violating after-state input (dizziness=true) that yields the lowered acuity self_care, versus emergent at the same assignment with dizziness=false.
